description: Maps every area of the repository to the code it owns and the documentation that governs it, so 'which docs govern this change?' always has a machine-readable answer. This file is committed data guarded by an admission test: scripts/check-area-map.js fails CI when the map goes stale (a pattern matching no tracked file, an entry pointing at an untracked file), when a tracked file resolves to no area, or when a doc under docs/ belongs to no area's doc set. Code patterns support '*', '**', and {a,b} alternation only, and every brace alternative must match at least one tracked file. Doc entries are literal tracked paths, never patterns. Code files and docs are many-to-many: a file or doc may belong to several areas. A code file may also name a governing doc directly with a '// see docs/<path>.md' comment, which adds every area whose doc set contains that doc.

repo-wide.description: Docs that apply to every area of the repository — a change anywhere can be governed by them. They resolve in their own right and do not need to belong to an area's doc set (though some also do).

unassigned.reason: License text — not project documentation and owned by no area.
Contributor license agreement — project governance, not area documentation.
Shipped end-user assets (icons, the reading guide delivered with dispatch payloads) — product artifacts, not project documentation.
